response.text:
ACCEPT

rationale: r1c5 is 10% below threshold with normal urgency; accepting 0.5 kWh transfer at reduced 0.10 kW/tick rate preserves our 28.7% SoC above critical floor while enabling mutual aid and reducing cascade risk during islanding.

system: You are the reactive subroutine of a household energy-coordination agent. Be brief and decisive.
user:
You are reacting to a REQUEST from r1c5. Payload: {'kwh': 0.5, 'urgency': 'normal'}. Their rationale: SoC frac 0.10 below share threshold; requesting energy via geographic circle..
Your current policy: sharing_intent=balanced, share_min_soc_frac=0.25, distrusted_peers=[].
Your latest belief: Islanded SoC 28.7% declining ~1.6 kWh/h. Neighbor r1c5 repeatedly requesting 0.5 kWh (normal urgency) over 2.5h; previous balanced policy (0.15 kW/tick) did not stabilize. Lowering share threshold to 25% and reducing max_share to 0.10 kW/tick to enable cautious mutual aid while preserving minimum survival margin. Reconnection signal absent; cascading collapse risk high. TTL 3 ticks to reassess if r1c5 accepts transfers or grid reconnects..
Reply with one of ACCEPT / REJECT / COUNTER on the first line, followed by `rationale: <one sentence>`.
